ground_truth.title: Top 15 video production tools for professionals [2025 edition]
ground_truth.author: Evelyn Trainor-Fogleman
ground_truth.main_content: Are you ready to finally put together your ultimate professional video production studio, but don't really know where to start? We're here to take you through the latest and greatest video production tools that will help you turn all those great ideas into great projects. First, let's start with the essentials: hardware. Any video production professional needs some hardware tools of the trade to get started. These include a video camera capable of shooting at least 1080p, but 4K is preferred; a PC or Mac with at least 8GB of RAM and a 1TB hard drive; a tripod; an external microphone; 2-3 external hard drives for storage and backups; and ideally, a large RAID backup drive that continually backs up all your files. Now we'll get into the real tools of the trade: software. Having lots of high-quality footage is great, but you're going to need software that can help you transform it from a bunch of loose video clips into a polished final project. You'll also need software to help you keep your projects and your team organized and in constant communication. Here are our top software tool picks for professional video producers: Video Editing Software (Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro X, Avid Media Composer), Graphics and Special Effects Software (After Effects, Photoshop), Sound Editing Software (Avid Pro Tools, Adobe Audition), File Sharing Software (Dropbox, Google Drive), Team Communication Software (Evercast, Slack, Microsoft Teams), and Mobile Apps (Adobe Premiere Rush, iMovie, FiLMiC Pro).
with: Are you ready to finally put together your ultimate professional video production studio, but don't really know where to start?
Any video production professional needs some hardware tools of the trade to get started.
Adobe Premiere Pro is one of the most popular video editing platforms available for video production professionals.
After Effects is almost undisputedly the most-loved visual effects program out there.
Pro Tools has long been an industry standard for professional mixing of everything from student films to Hollywood blockbusters.
